_comment: Archetype accomplice library for scope interrogation (Mechanism M2). Each archetype lists typical exogenous driver domains (accomplices) with a mechanism summary and a tentative prior. Domain-agnostic — covers markets, software, organizations, infrastructure, persons. Users may extend this file; new archetypes are discovered automatically by scope_auditor.py enumerate.

accomplices.mechanism: one-line mechanism

speculative_asset_market.name: Open-economy speculative asset market
speculative_asset_market.description: A market where asset prices are set by a thin pool of buyers with heterogeneous motivations, openness to cross-border capital, and a store-of-value use case.

accomplices.mechanism: foreign capital inflows create demand floor independent of local fundamentals
high-value durable assets are laundering vehicles; price support without local demand
citizenship/residency-by-investment programs convert legal status into demand
interest-rate regime and LTV caps change effective buyer pool size
new laws open or close channels (e.g. sanctions, golden-visa revocations)
remote-worker relocation and non-resident demographics shift demand mix
short-let yields reroute supply toward tourist economies
capital flight from sanctioned/unstable regions seeking safe-haven stores

regulated_infrastructure_service.name: Regulated infrastructure service
regulated_infrastructure_service.description: A capital-intensive service (power, water, telecom, rail) with natural-monopoly characteristics and a regulator as a primary stakeholder.

accomplices.mechanism: tariff caps, reliability standards, licensing regime as binding constraints
upstream fuel/material prices propagate via cost pass-through
weather and geophysical shocks drive demand and asset failure rates
subsidies and cross-subsidy mandates reshape unit economics
sector-wide labor agreements drive OPEX and reliability risk
cascading dependencies (e.g. grid on rail, water on power)

api_backed_software_service.name: API-backed software service
api_backed_software_service.description: A software product whose behavior depends on one or more external APIs, a hosting environment, and a community of integrators.

accomplices.mechanism: rate limits, schema changes, and SLAs propagate as behavior changes
region outages, billing model changes, platform deprecations drive availability
third-party clients expose surfaces not in the original product spec
SSO, OAuth, CA trust roots as hidden dependencies
GDPR/CCPA-class rules constrain storage, transit, and retention
abuse, spam, credential-stuffing shift observed load patterns

supply_chain_network.name: Supply-chain network
supply_chain_network.description: A multi-stage system where goods, services, or materials move between heterogeneous actors with transport, inventory, and timing constraints.

accomplices.mechanism: raw material availability and price drive downstream throughput
shipping routes, port congestion, freight rates as exogenous shocks
duties and sanctions redirect flows; rerouting creates new bottlenecks
transport costs tied to oil/gas prices
chokepoint disruptions (straits, canals) stop flows
end-consumer demand shifts ripple upstream with delay

information_market.name: Speculative information market
information_market.description: A venue where heterogeneous actors trade on asymmetric information about future events: prediction markets, betting markets, commodities futures, narrative-driven equities.

accomplices.mechanism: coordinated attention shifts create demand that is not grounded in fundamentals
algorithmic amplification selects which narratives reach critical mass
asymmetric information produces signature trade patterns before public events
SEC/CFTC/gambling-authority actions shut down or legitimize venues
market-maker behavior and latency regimes create non-fundamental signals

organizational_actor.name: Organizational actor
organizational_actor.description: A firm, agency, NGO, or coalition whose behavior emerges from the interaction of internal units, incentives, and external stakeholders.

accomplices.mechanism: rivals' moves drive defensive action and shape strategy
compliance mandates and investigations constrain action space
shareholder, donor, or parent-agency demands drive priorities
skill supply and wage pressures shape execution capacity
vendor actions cascade into the org's throughput
tax, monetary, trade policy reshape unit economics

individual_persona.name: Individual persona
individual_persona.description: A human subject whose behavior emerges from traits, immediate context, and broader life systems (family, work, finances, health).

accomplices.mechanism: domestic obligations and conflicts alter risk tolerance and timing
job pressure and ambition drive visible priorities
debts, windfalls, and cashflow constraints shape decisions
illness, fatigue, and medication alter behavior patterns
peer approval and reputation motivate otherwise irrational acts
group belonging dictates permissible positions

platform_ecosystem.name: Platform ecosystem
platform_ecosystem.description: A two- or multi-sided market where a platform operator mediates interactions between distinct user classes (buyers and sellers, creators and viewers, developers and users).

accomplices.mechanism: behavior on one side (e.g. sellers) constrains observed dynamics on the other (buyers)
ranking, fees, and policy enforcement reshape observed behavior
multi-homing and switching costs drive migration patterns
competition enforcement forces interop, fee caps, or breakups
underlying payment and KYC stacks gate access

research_knowledge_producer.name: Research / knowledge-producing institution
research_knowledge_producer.description: A lab, journal, think tank, or research network whose outputs emerge from funding, incentives, and intellectual lineages.

accomplices.mechanism: grantor priorities pre-select which questions get asked
publish-or-perish and citation metrics select for particular methods and topics
dominant paradigms filter acceptable framings
what data and tools are available shapes what can be measured
ethical review boards limit experimental designs

feedback_control_system.name: Feedback control system
feedback_control_system.description: An engineered or natural system with a sensed state, an actuator, a setpoint, and a control law — the archetype underlying thermostats, autopilots, biological homeostasis, and economic stabilization policies.

accomplices.mechanism: sensor noise and drift masquerade as system dynamics
unmodeled external forcing drives persistent error
physical limits of the actuator cap achievable control
human overrides and schedule changes create apparent control failure
cross-coupling between ostensibly independent loops creates emergent behavior
